documentation_dod.title: ggen Documentation Definition of Done
documentation_dod.description: Comprehensive specification of documentation completeness, validation, and evidence-first principles. All documentation must reference REAL code paths, REAL ontology, and ACTUAL file locations. No generic placeholders, no fabricated examples, no stale cross-references.
documentation_dod.core_principle: Evidence-First Rule: If you did not read it from the codebase or capture it from execution, do not write it.

1_architecture_docs.name: Architecture Documentation
1_architecture_docs.description: High-level system design, container diagrams, dependency maps, and cross-cutting patterns.
required_files: CLAUDE.md (root level, includes architecture reference section)
1_architecture_docs.source_of_truth: Live codebase (Cargo.toml members, LSP workspace survey, actual trait definitions)
1_architecture_docs.what_it_is: Single authoritative document explaining the 15-crate workspace, actual dependencies verified by LSP, real trait patterns, genuine error maps, and production sync flow

validation_checklist.expected: All 15 crates listed match docs/architecture/COMPRESSED_REFERENCE.md
validation_checklist.failure_means: COMPRESSED_REFERENCE.md is stale; must re-run LSP survey

validation_checklist.check: C4 Container Diagram is Actual (Not Template)
validation_checklist.command: LSP workspaceSymbol to verify crate dependencies
validation_checklist.expected: Diagram shows real dependencies (GCORE→GMKT, CLI→GCORE→GRECEIPT, etc.)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Diagram is generic or fabricated; must trace actual crate boundaries via LSP

validation_checklist.expected: Each crate has actual Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, CrateError>; quoted with real file paths
validation_checklist.failure_means: Patterns are generic examples; must cite actual implementations

validation_checklist.expected: All crate error enums listed with actual derive macros (#[derive(thiserror)])
validation_checklist.failure_means: Map is incomplete or generic; must enumerate all error types

validation_checklist.command: cargo make check && grep -rn 'pub fn run' crates/ggen-core/src/
validation_checklist.expected: Flow diagram shows actual function calls (μ₁ load, μ₂ extract, μ₃ generate, μ₄ validate, μ₅ emit)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Flow is theoretical; must trace actual execution paths from CLI entry point

forbidden: Generic C4 templates
Crate descriptions without Cargo.toml verification
Pattern names without actual code examples
Dependency diagrams not verified by LSP workspace survey
Error map without real enum definitions quoted
required: Real crate names verified against Cargo.toml
Actual trait/function signatures from lib.rs files
Genuine dependencies with call sites
Verified production sync flow with real stage names (μ₁–μ₅)
Error types with actual derive macros and field names
Last verified date (must be recent, e.g., 2026-04-01)
LSP survey methodology documented (e.g., 'workspaceSymbol on 15 crates')
update_trigger: Cargo.toml members change (add/remove crate)
New public trait/type added to any lib.rs
Error enum refactored (fields, variants added)
Cross-crate dependency introduced (check Cargo.toml depends_on)
Sync pipeline stages renamed
More than 3 months since last verification
1_architecture_docs.verification_command: just check && LSP workspaceSymbol <ggen-core> && diff Cargo.toml docs/architecture/COMPRESSED_REFERENCE.md | grep -i members

2_rules_documentation.name: Rules & Policy Documentation
2_rules_documentation.description: Modular, auto-loaded rules for development, testing, quality gates, and CI/CD discipline.
2_rules_documentation.source_of_truth: Live rules applied daily by agents and developers (not code artifacts)
2_rules_documentation.what_it_is: Policy and procedure manual enforced via hooks and validation gates. Covers: absolute rules (6 non-negotiable), workflow (4 steps: spec→test→ontology→commit), Rust practices (LSP, testing, performance), quality gates (Andon, OTEL validation), and failure modes (5 mistake classes, 6-question patch contract).

validation_checklist.expected: All .md files listed in .claude/rules/README.md with brief description
validation_checklist.failure_means: README is stale; add missing rule docs to index

validation_checklist.expected: 6 rows: Concurrent Operations, No Root Files, Task Tool, just Entry Point, TodoWrite Batch, Andon Protocol, ggen-lsp Diagnostics
validation_checklist.failure_means: Rules list incomplete; must enumerate all 6

validation_checklist.expected: Clear table of Grep vs LSP with specific tasks (find struct, list symbols, navigate modules)
validation_checklist.failure_means: LSP rule is vague; must provide concrete examples

validation_checklist.expected: >5 forbidden patterns explicitly listed with code blocks
validation_checklist.failure_means: Testing rules are generic; must show forbidden/allowed side-by-side

validation_checklist.expected: 3+ signal levels with example patterns (error[E...], test FAILED, warning, Clippy)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Signals incomplete; must enumerate actionable patterns

validation_checklist.expected: ≥5 mistake classes (Decorative Completion, Epistemic Bypass, Fail-Open, Legacy Contamination, Contract Drift)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Rule is incomplete; must cover all 5 classes with detection and forbidden examples

forbidden: Generic policy statements without enforcement mechanism
Rules that contradict each other (e.g., 'use LSP' but then 'Grep for symbols')
Examples without actual command output
Testing rules without forbidden/allowed code blocks
Absolute rules that are not enforced by hooks
Stale rule citations (e.g., referencing deleted files)
required: Each rule file has a version/last_updated date
README.md is complete index with brief descriptions
Absolute rules are numbered and cross-referenced in other docs
Every rule has at least one concrete example (not generic)
Testing rules show forbidden patterns with Rust code blocks
Andon signals have clear failure patterns and exit codes
OTEL validation has required spans table with attributes
Coding-agent mistakes have detection commands and forbidden examples
update_trigger: New development policy introduced (e.g., new gate, new testing requirement)
Absolute rule violated in production (add detection to prevent recurrence)
Agent failure mode discovered (add to coding-agent-mistakes.md)
Tooling changes (LSP update, new clippy rule, new cargo make recipe)
More than 2 months since last review
2_rules_documentation.verification_command: .claude/rules/README.md lists all .md files && each rule has last_updated date && Andon signals reference actual error patterns

3_api_documentation.name: API Documentation (Generated via cargo doc)
3_api_documentation.description: Rust doc comments on public types, traits, functions. Auto-generated HTML and searchable symbol index.
required_files: crates/*/src/*.rs (/// doc comments on public items)
3_api_documentation.source_of_truth: Rust source code (/// comments are authoritative)
3_api_documentation.what_it_is: Auto-generated API reference via `cargo doc` from doc comments in source. Includes type signatures, trait bounds, visibility, and examples blocks (validated by `cargo test --doc`).

validation_checklist.check: All Public Types Have Doc Comments
validation_checklist.command: cargo doc 2>&1 | grep -c 'warning: missing doc comment for'
validation_checklist.expected: 0 warnings for public items (must document all pub struct/enum/fn/trait)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Public API is undocumented; run `cargo doc` and add /// comments to missing items

validation_checklist.expected: All doctests pass (/// Examples blocks are executable Rust code)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Example is stale or broken; fix code block or mark with # ignore

validation_checklist.expected: No placeholder examples or stub documentation
validation_checklist.failure_means: Doc comments are incomplete; fill in real examples or move to COMPRESSED_REFERENCE.md

validation_checklist.command: cargo doc && grep -A 5 'pub trait' crates/*/src/lib.rs | head -20
validation_checklist.expected: Each trait has /// doc explaining purpose, examples, and generic bounds
validation_checklist.failure_means: Trait documentation is generic; add concrete examples with actual types

forbidden: Placeholder text in doc comments (e.g., 'TODO: document this')
Example blocks that don't compile (will fail `cargo test --doc`)
Generic descriptions without concrete types
Doc comments for private items (/// should only be on pub items)
Broken links in doc comments (use valid `path::to::Type` syntax)
required: All pub struct/enum/fn/trait have /// doc comments (no warnings on `cargo doc`)
Examples in doc comments are valid Rust code that compiles
Examples demonstrate the most common use case
Trait bounds and lifetimes are documented in comments
Links to related types use proper syntax ([`Type`](path::to::Type))
Errors returned by functions are documented
update_trigger: Public API changes (new type, new trait, signature change)
New error variant added
Trait bound changed
`cargo doc` produces new warnings
Example code is invalidated by refactoring
3_api_documentation.verification_command: cargo test --doc --all --exclude ggen-core && cargo doc --no-deps 2>&1 | grep -c 'warning:' | xargs test 0 -eq

4_examples_and_doctests.name: Examples & Validation (Real Executable Code)
4_examples_and_doctests.description: Working examples in doc comments and standalone example files that are validated by CI.
4_examples_and_doctests.source_of_truth: Live Rust code compiled by `cargo test --doc` and `cargo build --examples`
4_examples_and_doctests.what_it_is: Real, executable code examples that demonstrate APIs and workflows. All examples must compile and run as part of CI. No pseudocode, no YAML templates, no fabricated output.

location.doc_examples: crates/*/src/ (/// Examples blocks in doc comments)

validation_checklist.expected: All /// Examples blocks pass doctest compilation
validation_checklist.failure_means: Example is broken; fix or mark with # ignore (explain why)

validation_checklist.expected: No compilation errors
validation_checklist.failure_means: Example binary is broken; fix or delete

validation_checklist.expected: No placeholder implementations
validation_checklist.failure_means: Example is incomplete; either implement or delete

validation_checklist.expected: Output is actual program output, not fabricated
validation_checklist.failure_means: Example output is mocked; run real program and capture actual output

forbidden: Examples that use mock objects or test doubles
Pseudocode in example comments
Examples that don't compile (will fail `cargo test --doc`)
Fabricated example output (not from actual execution)
Examples that panic or exit with error (must handle Errors gracefully)
References to nonexistent functions or types
required: Every public function has at least one /// Examples block
Examples demonstrate the most common use case
Examples use real dependencies, not mocks
Examples handle Errors with Result<T, E>, not unwrap()
Output examples are captured from actual program run
Examples show input and output (not just code)
Standalone examples in examples/ are built and run by CI
update_trigger: Public API signature changes
New feature added
Example code breaks due to refactoring
`cargo test --doc` produces new failures

5_specification_docs.name: Specification Documentation (RDF-Driven Source)
5_specification_docs.description: RDF/Turtle ontology files (.specify/specs/) are the source of truth. Markdown files are GENERATED and must not be edited directly.
5_specification_docs.source_of_truth: RDF/Turtle files (.specify/specs/*/feature.ttl) — EDIT THESE, NOT THE .md FILES
5_specification_docs.what_it_is: Machine-readable specifications in RDF that drive code generation via μ₁–μ₅ pipeline. Markdown is auto-generated from RDF via `ggen sync`. NEVER hand-edit .md files; edit .ttl instead.

validation_checklist.expected: ✅ All SHACL constraints pass (no errors)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Feature violates ontology constraints; fix .ttl file

validation_checklist.expected: No differences (markdown is accurate reflection of RDF)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Markdown is stale; run `ggen sync` to regenerate

validation_checklist.expected: All required properties (label, comment, specification, philosophy) are present
validation_checklist.failure_means: Feature is incomplete; add missing RDF properties

validation_checklist.expected: All referenced ontology files exist and validate
validation_checklist.failure_means: Broken references; fix URIs or create missing .ttl files

validation_checklist.expected: No manual commits to .md files (only auto-generated by ggen sync)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Someone hand-edited .md; delete and regenerate via `ggen sync`

forbidden: Hand-editing .specify/specs/*/*.md (these are generated, not source)
RDF without SHACL validation (must pass `ggen validate`)
Specs without feature.ttl (RDF is required source)
Circular ontology references
Undefined namespace URIs (all prefixes must be declared)
Specs without corresponding code implementation (orphan specs)
required: Every spec has feature.ttl (RDF source, SHACL-valid)
RDF includes label, comment, specification, maturity, philosophy
All namespaces declared at top of .ttl file
Spec validates via `ggen validate` (0 errors)
Markdown generated from TTL via `ggen sync` (not hand-edited)
Cross-references to other specs use valid URIs
User stories, acceptance criteria, design constraints defined in RDF
Receipts generated for each `ggen sync` run
update_trigger: Feature requirements change (edit .ttl, run `ggen sync`)
New spec created (create .specify/specs/NNN-*/feature.ttl)
Ontology schema changes (update .specify/*.ttl)
Design decision recorded (add to RDF philosophy/notes)
`ggen validate` produces new errors or warnings

6_process_documentation.name: Process & Workflow Documentation
6_process_documentation.description: How ggen is developed, tested, released, and maintained. Includes gates, hooks, and CI/CD discipline.
6_process_documentation.source_of_truth: Live hooks and CI enforcement (.git/hooks/, justfile, cargo make recipes)
6_process_documentation.what_it_is: Developer workflow, quality gates, release gates, and failure modes. Covers: 4-step dev cycle (spec→test→ontology→commit), Andon signals (STOP on error), Definition of Done (check→lint→test→slo-check all pass).

validation_checklist.expected: At least 15 crate names mentioned in CLAUDE.md
validation_checklist.failure_means: CLAUDE.md is stale; update crate list and architecture section

validation_checklist.expected: 4 numbered steps: Create RDF Spec, Chicago TDD, Generate from Ontology, Commit with Evidence
validation_checklist.failure_means: Workflow is incomplete; document all 4 steps

validation_checklist.command: just check && just lint && just test-unit && just slo-check
validation_checklist.expected: All 4 gates pass (0 compiler errors, 0 warnings, all tests pass, SLOs met)
validation_checklist.failure_means: DoD gate is broken; fix failing step or update gate definition

validation_checklist.expected: Pre-commit hook enforces Andon protocol (fails on errors, not silently ignored)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Andon enforcement is missing; add error checks to pre-commit hook

validation_checklist.expected: Release gate script exists with validation checks
validation_checklist.failure_means: Release gate is missing; implement verification and rollout process

forbidden: Workflow steps that are not enforced by hooks
Gates that can be bypassed (no --no-verify, no skip options)
Stale process documentation (e.g., references deleted files or commands)
Definition of Done without all required checks
Andon signals without actionable failure messages
Release gates without evidence collection
required: CLAUDE.md is current (version, architecture section up-to-date)
4-step workflow is documented in .claude/rules/_core/workflow.md
Definition of Done checklist is in .claude/rules/README.md
All dev commands are in justfile (not bare cargo)
Pre-commit hook enforces checks (no --no-verify bypass)
Pre-push hook runs tests (gate blocks incomplete work)
Release gate validates before shipping (OTEL, receipts, tests)
Evidence is collected at each stage (receipts, test output, spans)
update_trigger: New development policy or gate introduced
Workflow step changes (e.g., new SHACL validation before commit)
Tool changes (new cargo make recipe, new linter, new test framework)
Gate becomes unreliable (false positives/negatives)
Release process needs validation update
6_process_documentation.verification_command: just check && just lint && just test && just slo-check && cat CLAUDE.md | grep 'ggen-core' | wc -l | xargs test 1 -le

7_evidence_first_principle.name: Evidence-First Principle (MANDATORY)
7_evidence_first_principle.description: All documentation must reference REAL code paths, REAL OTEL output, and ACTUAL execution evidence. No fabricated examples, no generic placeholders, no theoretical claims without proof.
7_evidence_first_principle.what_it_is: Constitutional rule that documentation is only valid if it can be corroborated by reading actual code or capturing real execution. Prevents NARRATION failures (claiming completion without proof).

validation_checklist.expected: 0 matches (no placeholder examples)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Documentation contains generic examples; replace with real code paths

validation_checklist.check: File Paths Are Absolute and Real
validation_checklist.expected: 0 matches (all paths are actual absolute paths)
validation_checklist.failure_means: Docs contain placeholder paths; use real /home/user/ggen/crates/... paths

validation_checklist.command: for quote in $(grep -rn 'crate.*Error' docs/ | grep -o '"[^"]*"' | head -3); do grep -q "$quote" crates/*/src/*.rs || echo "Stale: $quote"; done
validation_checklist.expected: All quoted code snippets exist in actual source files
validation_checklist.failure_means: Quoted code is stale or fabricated; update quotes from real source

validation_checklist.check: OTEL Examples Captured from Real Runs
validation_checklist.expected: OTEL span examples are from actual RUST_LOG=trace runs (not synthetic)
validation_checklist.failure_means: OTEL output is fabricated; run real code and capture actual spans

validation_checklist.expected: Claims are followed by evidence ('verified by', 'captured from', 'confirmed by')
validation_checklist.failure_means: Documentation asserts without proving; add evidence citation

forbidden: Fabricating JSON schemas, API responses, or OTEL traces
Generic 5 Whys analysis without real failure data
Template-filled documentation (copy-paste boilerplate)
Claiming features work without OTEL evidence
Pseudocode presented as real code
Example output not captured from actual execution
File paths with placeholders (e.g., `/path/to/file`)
Theoretical claims without empirical corroboration
required: Every code quote must be from actual source (not paraphrased)
Every OTEL example must be from RUST_LOG=trace execution
Every file path must be absolute and real (verified to exist)
Every claim must be corroborated by reading code or capturing output
Every example must be executable as-is (not pseudocode)
Every error message must be from actual error output (not made up)
Every design decision must reference the RDF spec that drove it
Every architecture claim must be verified by LSP workspace survey
7_evidence_first_principle.how_to_apply: Before writing any documentation, follow this workflow: (1) Read the code (actual files); (2) Run the feature (capture real output); (3) Verify claims (OTEL spans, test passes); (4) Document with evidence (quote code, cite file paths, embed real output); (5) Cross-check (run commands to verify all paths and quotes exist). If you didn't read it or capture it, don't write it.
update_trigger: Any documentation written without reading source
Any claim made without OTEL/test corroboration
Any file path that is generic or placeholder
Any code quote not from actual source
Any example output not from real execution

8_searchability_and_navigation.name: Searchability & Navigation (Cross-Linked Index)
8_searchability_and_navigation.description: All documentation is indexed, discoverable, and cross-linked. Single entry point (CLAUDE.md or docs/INDEX.md) navigates to all surfaces.
required_files: All .md files have internal links [Rule Name](../rules/file.md)
8_searchability_and_navigation.source_of_truth: Live file structure and links (must point to actual files)
8_searchability_and_navigation.what_it_is: Navigable documentation with single entry point and consistent cross-references. Users start at CLAUDE.md and can reach any surface via breadcrumbs and links.

validation_checklist.command: grep -o '\[.*\](.*\.md)' CLAUDE.md | sed 's/.*(//' | sed 's/)//' | while read f; do test -f "$f" || echo "BROKEN: $f"; done
validation_checklist.expected: All links in CLAUDE.md point to existing files
validation_checklist.failure_means: CLAUDE.md has broken links; update paths to actual files

validation_checklist.expected: All .md files in docs/ are listed in docs/INDEX.md
validation_checklist.failure_means: INDEX.md is incomplete; add missing doc files to index

validation_checklist.expected: >10 links to individual rule files
validation_checklist.failure_means: README.md is not a proper index; add links to all rule files

validation_checklist.command: find docs .claude -name '*.md' -exec grep -l '\[.*\](.*\.md)' {} \; | xargs -I {} bash -c 'grep -o "\\[.*\\](.*\\.md)" {} | sed "s/.*(/$(dirname {})/" | sed "s/).*/\\1/" | while read f; do test -f "\$f" || echo "BROKEN in {}: \$f"; done'
validation_checklist.expected: 0 broken links across all documentation
validation_checklist.failure_means: Some links are stale; find and fix broken file references

validation_checklist.expected: Each doc has back-link or breadcrumb to parent/home
validation_checklist.failure_means: Navigation is incomplete; add breadcrumbs to long docs

forbidden: Broken internal links (doc exists but path is wrong)
Circular links (A→B→A without escape)
Links to placeholder files (links to `/path/to/file`)
Duplicated content (same info in multiple places without links)
Missing entry point (users can't find where to start)
Orphaned documents (files not reachable from CLAUDE.md)
required: CLAUDE.md is the single entry point for all documentation
All links in CLAUDE.md point to existing files (no broken links)
docs/INDEX.md lists all major documentation files
.claude/rules/README.md is a complete index of rule files
Each documentation file has a clear purpose statement
Cross-references use relative paths (e.g., ../rules/testing.md)
No circular dependencies in link graph
Every .md file is reachable from CLAUDE.md within 3 clicks
update_trigger: New documentation file created (add to INDEX.md and CLAUDE.md)
Documentation file renamed or moved (update all internal links)
Documentation file deleted (remove from indexes)
New rule added to .claude/rules/ (add to README.md)
Architecture documentation updated (verify all links still work)
8_searchability_and_navigation.verification_command: grep -o '\[.*\](.*\.md)' CLAUDE.md docs/INDEX.md .claude/rules/README.md | sed 's/.*(//' | sed 's/)//' | sort | uniq | while read f; do test -f "$f" && echo "✓ $f" || echo "✗ BROKEN: $f"; done

validation_checklist_master.description: Master checklist to validate entire documentation system against Definition of Done
all_surfaces_pass: just check && just lint && just test && just slo-check ✅
grep -rn 'TODO.*doc\|placeholder' docs/ crates/*/src/ | wc -l | xargs test 0 -eq ✅
grep -o '\[.*\](.*\.md)' CLAUDE.md | sed 's/.*(//' | sed 's/)//' | while read f; do test -f $f || exit 1; done ✅
validation_checklist_master.failure_response: If ANY check fails: (1) Identify surface (2) Read failure (3) Fix root cause (4) Re-run check (5) Do NOT proceed until ALL checks pass

governance.enforcement: Pre-commit hook verifies: no fabricated docs, no broken links, all CLAUDE.md references valid

implementation_example.scenario: Adding a new crate to ggen

steps.action: Run LSP workspaceSymbol to discover types/traits

steps.docs_impact: Real examples for doc comments, OTEL spans captured

steps.action: Verify: just check && cargo doc && ggen validate && all links work
